_note: Artem translation b01-b03 — first 3 items for next Translate RU→EN slot. Targets the 3 A2 PVs that failed cold production in the 2026-05-03 foundation test: go on (substituted with 'happening'), look for (confused with 'look after'), come back (wrong direction — used 'go back' from destination POV). Business context where natural. Articles stripped by normalize() in s90r2.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-05-03 A1-A2 production test #3: substituted non-PV 'happening' for target 'going on'. PV-avoidance pattern.
correct_answers: What's going on in the meeting
What is going on in the meeting
What's going on at the meeting
What is going on at the meeting

common_errors.feedback: ✓ 'What's happening' is grammatical English, but the target PV is 'going on'. In meetings/teams the PV form is the natural register: 'what's going on with the timeline?', 'something's going on with the budget'. You substituted a non-PV — that's the avoidance pattern we're drilling out.
'Taking place' is correct but overly formal for a meeting. Target the conversational PV: 'going on'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'What's going on in the meeting?' The PV is 'go on' (= happen, be in progress). Practise reaching for it instead of 'happening'.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-05-03 A1-A2 test #4: produced 'looked after for keys' — blend of 'look for' (search) and 'look after' (care for). Particle confusion within same base verb.
exercises.prompt_ru: Я ищу твой имейл с прошлой недели.
correct_answers: I'm looking for your email from last week
I am looking for your email from last week
I'm looking for the email from last week
I am looking for the email from last week

common_errors.pattern_label: look after (caregiving) instead of look for (search)
common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'Look after' = take care of (children, pets, plants). Russian 'искать' = search for, which is 'look FOR'. Fix the particle: ✓ 'looking FOR your email'. The PV pair is the trap — same base verb, opposite domains.
✗ 'Looking your email' — English 'look' as a search verb requires 'for'. ✓ 'looking FOR your email'.
'Search' takes 'for' too: 'searching FOR your email'. But the target PV here is 'look for' — practise that one.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'I'm looking for your email from last week.' PV is 'look for' (search). Don't confuse with 'look after' (take care of).

exercises.source_observation: 2026-05-03 A1-A2 test #1 + #10: used 'go back' / 'come back' from wrong speaker POV. Russian 'вернуться' is single verb; English splits by destination POV.
correct_answers: Come back soon, we're waiting for you
Come back soon, we are waiting for you
Come back quickly, we're waiting for you

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'Go back' is wrong here. The 'we're waiting for you' anchors the speaker AT the destination — the listener is moving TOWARD the speaker. Use ✓ 'COME back'. Rule: come back = toward speaker; go back = away from speaker.
'Return' is correct but formal/written register. In spoken/casual: 'come back'.
✗ 'waiting you' — direct calque of 'ждём тебя'. English 'wait' always needs 'for'. ✓ 'waiting FOR you'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'Come back soon, we're waiting for you.' Speaker is at the destination → 'come back'. If speaker were elsewhere → 'go back'.
